samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse detection module detecting transitions from 0 to 1 back to 0 over three cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Input clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating detected pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    
    <parameter_description>
      <state id="S0">Initial state.</state>
      <state id="S1">State after data_in = 1.</state>
      <state id="S2">State after data_in = 0.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
      <register id="state">Stores the current state of the pulse detection process.</register>
      
      <always_comb>
        // Logic to determine next_state based on current state and inputs
        case(state)
          S0 : begin
            if(data_in == 1'b0)
              next_state <= S0;
            else
              next_state <= S1;
          end
          
          S1 : begin
            if(data_in == 1'b1)
              next_state <= S1;
            else
              next_state <= S2;
          end
            
          S2 : begin
            if(data_in == 1'b0)
              next_state <= S0;
            else
              next_state <= S1;
          end
      
          default: begin
            next_state <= S0;
          end
        
        endcase
      </always_comb>
      
      <always_ff posedge clk or negedge rst_n>
        // State register update logic
        if(~rst_n)
          state <= S0;
        
        else
          state <= next_state;
      
      </always_ff>
      
      <assign data_out = ((state == S0 && next_state == S1) || (state == S1 && next_state == S2)) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;  // Set data_out high if moving from S1 to S2
    
    </logic_description>
</module>
